reasoning: The document shows strong content preservation with high shingling metrics and a high LLM composite score. However, a major structural defect was identified where bulleted lists inside table cells were extracted as plain text with literal bullet characters rather than properly structured HTML lists. Following the count-based routing thresholds, the presence of one major defect requires HUMAN_REVIEW.

concerns.analysis: The source document contains several bulleted lists under the agenda topics (e.g., under 'Project Finance Update' there are bullets for 'Cost share payments received.' and 'Funds expended.'). In the output JSON, these bulleted items are extracted as plain text with literal bullet characters (•) separated by newline characters inside the table cell, instead of being structured as proper list elements. This breaks the structural semantics of the list for accessibility.
concerns.source_pdf_quote:
Project Finance Update
• Cost share payments received.
• Funds expended.
concerns.output_quote: "Project Finance Update\n• Cost share payments received.\n• Funds expended."
concerns.location: Row 1, Column 1 of the schedule table

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 1 table(s) have time values in <th> cells: "10:00-10:10"
concerns.analysis: data_in_th_time=1, no_th_tables=0. Minor semantic markup issues: tables lack <th> elements or use time values as headers. These affect screen reader context but are less severe than data misrepresentation.

action_items: Convert the plain text bullet points with literal bullets into proper HTML list elements (<ul>/<li>) within the respective table cells.
Review the table header structure to ensure that <th> elements contain appropriate semantic headers rather than data values.
